TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 4. At the conclusion of this Conference, we reaffirm the paramount importance of education in our national development policies. Consequently, we call for the mobilization of all partners to be able to meet the immense challenges of youth education for a human-faced globalization and sustainable development of our societies and the entire planet. Only new alliances and effective partnerships will make it possible to find diverse and appropriate responses to the current and future needs of young people and to improve the quality of their education. These alliances must be built and strengthened within the States themselves, among governments, civil society, the economic sector, the media, and the youth themselves. They must also be realized and reinforced at regional and international levels, among all development partners so that the commitments made by the international community to support the improvement of education quality can be fulfilled.

low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes partnerships across governments, civil society, the economic sector, and international development partners, directly referencing these stakeholders. 'Government and policy makers' are highlighted through national development policies. 'Civil society and NGOs', 'Private sector and industry', and 'International organizations and donors' are explicitly named as key alliance partners. 'Learners and students' and 'Parents and communities' are not directly mentioned, so they are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes improving education quality (Quality and learning outcomes), calls for partnerships across sectors and levels (International cooperation and partnerships), and mentions building alliances within and across states (Governance and planning). 'Policy theme' is broad, but the text specifies systemic improvements and partnerships, excluding other categories like access or financing.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes partnerships for sustainable development (Sustainable development and climate action), inclusive alliances across sectors (Inclusive education and special needs), and global cooperation for human-faced globalization (Global citizenship and peace education). 'Policy theme' and 'Learning modality' are excluded as the text focuses on collaboration frameworks, not specific pedagogical methods or policy types.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes building alliances among governments, civil society, the economic sector, media, and youth, indicating collaboration between public and private entities. 'Public-private partnership (PPP)' directly aligns with this multi-stakeholder collaboration. Other categories like 'Public' or 'Private non-profit' are excluded as the focus is on partnerships rather than singular ownership types.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 5. We also express the hope that the Conclusions and Priority Proposals for Action, which we adopted unanimously on September 11, 2004, may genuinely become a source of inspiration for our educational policies and serve to bring about a strong and lasting improvement in the quality of education for all young people worldwide, for peace, justice, and sustainable development.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes improving education quality for peace, justice, and sustainable development, aligning with 'Quality and learning outcomes' and 'Sustainable development and environment'. 'Policy theme' was considered but narrowed to these specific areas due to the focus on quality improvement and sustainable development goals.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'peace, justice, and sustainable development' as aspirational values for education policies, aligning with 'Global citizenship and peace education' and 'Sustainable development and climate action'. 'Policy theme' was considered but excluded as the focus is on values rather than direct policy mechanisms.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 6. Impressive figures: Currently, half of the world’s population is under the age of 25. The world is home to the largest youth generation it has ever known. More than one billion people are between the ages of 12 and 20. By 2020, 87% of young people worldwide will live in developing countries, where they are already the majority today. Meanwhile, developed countries will face the demographic challenge of an increasingly aging population.

low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes youth demographics, mentioning 12-20 year olds and the global youth generation, supporting 'Youth and adolescents'. It also references 'half of the world’s population under 25' and 'children' explicitly, justifying 'Children (0-17)'. Other categories like 'Adults' or 'Disadvantaged groups' lack direct evidence.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 7. Broader access and opportunities, but still significant challenges: The number of young people completing primary education is continuously increasing. The number of enrollments in secondary education worldwide has multiplied tenfold over the past 50 years. While global secondary school enrollment rates have risen from 56% to 77.5% during the last decade, this average conceals the reality of millions of young people, particularly in the Global South, who, despite finishing primary school, are still deprived of the opportunity to earn a living or continue their education. Moreover, a large number of them have not even completed primary education. Girls are especially affected.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions primary education completion and secondary enrollment rates, directly referencing ISCED 1 and ISCED 2/3. 'Multiple levels' was excluded as the text specifies distinct primary and secondary tiers, not a broad multi-level category.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph discusses primary and secondary education access, implicating children and youth. Girls are explicitly highlighted as a vulnerable group. Low-income groups are inferred through references to 'Global South' and systemic barriers. 'Learners with disabilities' and 'Indigenous or minority groups' are not explicitly mentioned, so they are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes disparities in education access (Access and equity) and highlights gender-specific challenges (Inclusion and diversity). 'Quality and learning outcomes' is excluded as the focus is on access, not learning results. 'Policy theme' is not selected as a sub-label per instructions, and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' is omitted due to lack of explicit policy framing in the text.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph provides enrollment statistics (primary/secondary education rates) and highlights systemic challenges in education access, aligning with 'Education statistics and indicators' and 'Education system monitoring & evaluation'. 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' and 'Infrastructure & resources' are excluded as the text focuses on statistical trends and systemic barriers rather than specific infrastructure or gender as a cross-cutting theme.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 8. Improving the quality of education for the twenty-first century: Too many young people do not have the opportunity to acquire the knowledge, attitudes, and values necessary to live in the twenty-first century. There is a growing demand for education to be more relevant to the modern world and for individuals to continue learning throughout their lives. Consequently, the issues related to the quality of education for this age group have now become central to national and international priorities.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes modernizing education to align with 21st-century needs, focusing on knowledge, attitudes, and values rather than specific vocational skills. While 'Combined general and vocational' might seem relevant due to mention of 'lifelong learning,' the text prioritizes academic relevance over technical training. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded as the paragraph does not specifically address job-specific skills or technical education.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes improving education quality for the 21st century and the need for lifelong learning, directly aligning with 'Quality and learning outcomes' and 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. Other categories like 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' and 'Governance and planning' are less directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes 21st-century relevance, lifelong learning, and modern competencies required for contemporary life, directly aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies.' Other categories like digital literacy or civic education are not explicitly mentioned. The focus on 'knowledge, attitudes, and values' for modern living supports this selection over narrower themes.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 9. Gender equality: In most regions of the world, there are unacceptable gender disparities in access to and retention in education, learning opportunities, and their outcomes. This situation reflects the limited capacity of many education systems to be sufficiently responsive to gender issues.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph highlights gender disparities in access to education (Access and equity), outcomes of learning opportunities (Quality and learning outcomes), and systemic issues affecting inclusivity (Inclusion and diversity). Governance and other categories are less directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions gender disparities and education systems' limited responsiveness to gender issues, aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. The mention of gender as a cross-cutting theme affecting education systems, despite debate, justifies 'Other cross-cutting theme'. Categories like 'Inclusive education and special needs' are excluded as the focus is on gender disparities broadly, not specific inclusion needs.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions gender disparities affecting women and girls, directly linking to the 'Women and girls' category. It also references 'unacceptable gender disparities' which often correlate with low-income and disadvantaged groups, supporting that category. Other categories like 'All learners' or 'Indigenous or minority groups' are not specifically mentioned in the text.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 10. Life skills: Currently, education systems, including technical and vocational education and training, do not provide young people with the opportunity to acquire and strengthen the complex skills necessary for life. Many of these skills are essential to overcoming contemporary challenges such as HIV/AIDS and other pandemics, transitioning from education to the labor market, as well as social and political disengagement. The lack of technical and vocational training, including entrepreneurial spirit and the desire to create, hinders the possibility of finding employment.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'life skills' and links them to pandemic resilience (health), labor market transition, and social engagement. 'Entrepreneurship' is directly referenced in the context of technical training. 'Health and well-being education' aligns with pandemic challenges like HIV/AIDS. Other categories lack direct textual support.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes gaps in acquiring life skills (Quality and learning outcomes), links skills to pandemic resilience (Health and well-being policies), and mentions transition to labor market (Lifelong learning and adult education). Curriculum/ pedagogy and policy theme were excluded as the focus is on skill gaps, not curriculum design or systemic policy frameworks.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 11. Social inclusion: In an increasingly shrinking world due to globalization and information and communication technologies, exclusion is on the rise. Factors such as poverty, gender, abilities, origin, language, culture, and various forms of discrimination can constitute obstacles to effective learning.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph directly addresses discrimination based on gender, abilities, origin, etc., aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. 'Inclusive education and special needs' matches the focus on barriers to learning for marginalized groups. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is used as the paragraph's emphasis on systemic exclusion and its educational implications doesn't fully fit existing categories.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions poverty, gender, abilities, and origin as barriers to learning, directly aligning with Low-income and disadvantaged groups, Women and girls, Learners with disabilities, and Indigenous or minority groups. Other categories like Children or Adults are not specified, and 'Population in conflict' is not mentioned.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 12. Conditions facilitating the lives of teachers: In many parts of the world, training, resources, support, materials, and adequate working conditions are lacking for teachers and trainers. As a result, they cannot be effective in guiding the learning of young people.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph highlights lack of training, resources, and working conditions, aligning with 'Initial education and training' and 'Working conditions and salaries'. 'Teacher supply and shortages' is excluded as the focus is on support gaps, not numbers.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'resources, support, materials' which directly maps to 'Teaching and learning materials'. 'Classrooms and learning environments' aligns with 'adequate working conditions' and 'training' implied in the context of physical and functional learning spaces. 'School buildings' and 'ICT' are not explicitly mentioned, while 'WASH' and 'transport' are irrelevant here.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions teachers and trainers facing systemic resource gaps, directly aligning with 'Teachers and educators'. Other categories like 'Government and policy makers' are excluded as the focus is on teacher conditions, not policy formulation. 'Learners and students' are indirectly affected but not the primary stakeholders discussed.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 13. Anticipating the future: The rapid and unpredictable changes in today’s world have rendered the current provision of education insufficiently appropriate.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes adapting education to future changes, aligning with 'General (academic)' as it addresses broad educational adequacy. 'Vocational/technical' is excluded as the focus is on general adaptability, not specific skills. 'Combined' is rejected since the text does not explicitly mention integration of general and vocational aspects.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes future-ready education and adaptability to rapid changes, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' for adaptability and 'Digital and ICT literacy' for navigating technological changes. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as specific categories better capture the focus.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 14. Resource allocation: In many regions of the world, the educational needs of young people are not always a priority for economic and social reasons. Consequently, there are insufficient resources allocated to this important age group, both by countries and by the entire community of development partners.

low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph discusses insufficient resources allocated to education, but does not specify school buildings, classrooms, materials, ICT, labs, libraries, WASH, or transport. 'Other' is the only category that broadly captures resource allocation concerns without over-specifying. Other categories are too concrete to fit the general mention of resource allocation.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph directly addresses insufficient resource allocation by countries and development partners, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also mentions systemic issues in prioritizing educational needs, supporting 'Governance and planning'. 'International cooperation and partnerships' is relevant but not explicitly stated. 'Other policy theme' is avoided due to specific categories fitting.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 15. Alliances and partnerships: The mobilization of effective alliances for quality education for all young people is insufficient among students, teachers, parents, communities, civil society, the media, businesses, the community as a whole, and stakeholders. In particular, in many situations, young people do not have enough opportunities to be active partners in the development and evolution of their education.

low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions young people (learners/students) lacking participation, parents and communities as stakeholders, and civil society as underutilized partners. These categories directly align with the text's focus on stakeholder engagement gaps. Government and private sector are mentioned but not as primary focus areas. Other categories are not explicitly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph mentions 'communities' and 'stakeholders' as underrepresented in education partnerships, aligning with 'Community or faith-based'. 'Public-private partnership (PPP)' is implied by the reference to 'alliances' and 'partnerships' involving multiple sectors. 'Public' and 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the focus is on collaborative efforts rather than single-sector provision.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 16. Peace, sustainable development, and social justice: Young people must have access to quality education and training in order to expand their capacity to be agents of sustainable development and builders of a world of peace and justice. Education must reflect the nature of cultures and languages, the value of the individual in a broader context, as well as the importance of living in a way that promotes equality and encourages a non-violent and sustainable future.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes peace, sustainable development, and social justice, aligning with 'Global citizenship and peace education' through non-violence and cultural relevance. 'Human rights and equality' matches the focus on equality and individual value. 'Sustainable development and climate action' corresponds to the sustainable future theme. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as specific categories better capture the content.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes sustainable development and non-violence, aligning with 'Sustainable development and environment'. Cultural relevance and equality themes support 'Inclusion and diversity'. Access to quality education is explicitly mentioned, fitting 'Access and equity'. Specific curriculum/pedagogy terms are excluded as per the summary's guidance.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes young people's role in sustainable development and peace, directly supporting 'Youth and adolescents'. The reference to 'all cultures and languages' and 'individual in a broader context' suggests inclusivity, aligning with 'All learners'. Other categories like 'Children' are excluded as the focus is on youth, and specific groups aren't mentioned.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 17. Develop a comprehensive strategy. To achieve development, peace, and justice globally, it is essential to implement a comprehensive strategy that ensures the learning needs of all young people are met through equitable access to learning programs that promote the acquisition of life skills and adequate training encompassing all aspects of education quality.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'life skills' and 'equitable access to learning programs', aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' and 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the emphasis on comprehensive strategy and education quality, which don't fit other categories directly.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes equitable access to learning programs (Access and equity), education quality through life skills and training (Quality and learning outcomes), and curriculum/pedagogy linked to education quality (Curriculum and pedagogy policies). 'Governance' and 'Financing' are excluded as not explicitly mentioned, while 'Inclusion' is narrower than the paragraph's focus on access and equity.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes developing a comprehensive strategy (Design and development), promoting life skills and education quality (Content and learning areas), and ensuring equitable access to learning programs (Curriculum implementation). 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or reform. 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' is not explicitly addressed. 'Other curriculum-related issue' is avoided due to specificity.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 18. Increase access and equity for all young people. New ways of designing education, involving creative organizational and pedagogical methods and the use of ICT, must be implemented to increase access and retention of young people in education. It is important to recognize the correlation between formal and non-formal learning. Governments are encouraged to establish systems for the validation of non-formal learning.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph explicitly mentions formal and non-formal learning as correlated, and validates non-formal learning. 'Lifelong learning' is implied through the emphasis on access, retention, and systems for validation. 'Informal learning' is excluded as the text does not discuss unstructured or incidental learning.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes new pedagogical methods (teaching methods and approaches), ICT use (technology-supported pedagogy), and validation systems for non-formal learning (assessment and feedback). 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' is excluded as the text focuses on implementation rather than theoretical frameworks. 'Professional learning communities' is not mentioned. 'Other pedagogy related issue' is avoided due to specificity requirements.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes increasing access and equity for young people, directly matching 'Access and equity'. It mentions 'new ways of designing education' and 'use of ICT' which align with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' and 'Innovation and technology policies'. 'Governance and planning' was excluded as the focus is on implementation methods rather than institutional structures.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 19. Improve the methods of innovation and creation. Governments, teachers and trainers, as well as all other stakeholders involved, should promote renewed national objectives focused on the relevance of education in the 21st century. This involves fostering the strengthening, evaluation, innovation, diversification, and reform of current educational delivery methods and their appropriateness in each context, particularly those characterized by poverty and post-conflict countries.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph mentions 'relevance of education in the 21st century' and 'innovation, diversification, and reform of current educational delivery methods', aligning with 'General (academic)' and 'Combined general and vocational' which cover both academic and practical aspects. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded as the focus is broader than just technical skills.
low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph emphasizes innovation in educational delivery methods, diversification, and context-specific reforms, aligning with non-formal education's focus on flexible, context-appropriate learning. Informal learning is supported by the mention of post-conflict and poverty contexts, which often rely on informal pathways. Lifelong learning is implied through the call for continuous reform and relevance in the 21st century. Formal education is excluded as the text prioritizes adaptive, non-traditional methods over structured formal systems.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes innovation, diversification, and reform of educational delivery methods (Teaching methods and approaches). It also mentions strengthening and evaluation of methods, aligning with Pedagogical foundations and theories. The reference to context-specific reforms and stakeholder collaboration points to Other pedagogy related issue. Assessment and feedback are not explicitly addressed, and technology is not mentioned, so those categories are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions governments, teachers, and trainers as key stakeholders. 'Other stakeholders' is included due to the broad reference to 'all other stakeholders involved.' Parents, communities, civil society, private sector, and international organizations are not specifically named in the text.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes 21st-century education relevance, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. Mention of poverty and post-conflict contexts supports 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included due to the focus on context-specific reforms and stakeholder collaboration not fully captured by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes innovation in educational delivery methods (Innovation and technology policies), curriculum reform and context-specific approaches (Curriculum and pedagogy policies), and mentions post-conflict contexts (Education in emergencies and crises). Governance and planning is included due to references to national objectives and stakeholder collaboration. Excluded categories like access and equity are not explicitly addressed here.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 20. Take decisive action to address gender inequality. In many situations, it will be necessary to undertake concerted and positive actions aimed at compensating for historical and contemporary inequalities. A substantial body of research, knowledge, and good practices exists, and these must be prioritized to generalize gender equality at the national, regional, and global levels.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes gender equality as a human rights issue and highlights the need for systemic action and knowledge application, aligning with 'Human rights and equality'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits due to the focus on actionable strategies and research-based practices. 'Other cross-cutting theme' was excluded as specific categories better capture the content.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes addressing gender inequality through systemic action and multi-level governance, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity' for gender equality focus. 'Governance and planning' matches the mention of national, regional, and global implementation. Other categories like 'Policy theme' are excluded as per the summary's guidance to avoid overlap, and 'Legal frameworks' is not mentioned.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 21. Establish strategies that prioritize life skills. To learn how to solve problems and take action, skills must be better defined, particularly for preventing HIV/AIDS, enhancing employability, and fostering active citizenship. Proven practices include conflict resolution, peacebuilding, and cultivating the desire to read and write meaningful texts.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'life skills' and links them to HIV/AIDS prevention (health), conflict resolution/peacebuilding (global citizenship), and active citizenship (peace education). '21st century competencies' aligns with problem-solving and action-taking. Other categories like digital literacy or economic literacy are not mentioned, and 'inclusive education' is not addressed.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes specific life skills (e.g., conflict resolution, peacebuilding) and learning outcomes (e.g., preventing HIV/AIDS, enhancing employability), aligning with 'Content and learning areas' and 'Competency-based and learning outcomes'. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' are not explicitly addressed, and other categories lack direct textual support.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 22. Focus on the most common reasons for exclusion. It is essential to take action to identify the factors that exclude young people from learning.

low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'young people' and focuses on factors excluding them from learning, directly aligning with 'Youth and adolescents'. Other categories like 'Children (0-17)' are excluded as the emphasis is on youth, not children. Categories related to disabilities, gender, or conflict settings are not mentioned, so they are omitted.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes identifying factors that exclude young people, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity' and 'Access and equity'. 'Policy theme' is contested in the summary, but the paragraph's focus on exclusion factors directly supports these two categories. Other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Governance' are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 23. Recognize the importance of teachers and trainers. It is necessary to establish increased opportunities for the continuous professional development of teachers. Furthermore, working conditions, career prospects, and salaries that make the teaching profession attractive should be ensured to strengthen the social status of teachers. Effective management is required to support teachers.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'continuous professional development' (Professional development), 'working conditions, career prospects, and salaries' (Working conditions and salaries), and 'effective management' which relates to evaluation/performance (Teacher evaluation and performance). 'Recruitment and hiring' and 'qualification and certification' are not directly addressed. 'Teacher supply and shortages' is irrelevant here.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on teachers' professional development, working conditions, and management, directly aligning with 'Teachers and workforce policies'. 'Governance and planning' applies due to mentions of effective management and systemic support. 'Quality and learning outcomes' is excluded as the focus is on workforce rather than learning results. 'Policy theme' is not selected as the sub-labels better capture specific aspects.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions teachers and their professional development, working conditions, and career prospects, directly aligning with 'Teachers and educators'. 'Government and policy makers' is included due to the mention of policy commitments and the need for effective management, which implies governmental or institutional responsibility. Other categories like 'Learners and students' or 'Parents and communities' are not mentioned, and 'Pedagogy & assessment' is not addressed.

paragraph: 24. Utilize existing knowledge and promote research. In the development of educational policies, forecasts derived from research and alternative future trends should be taken into account in order to make informed choices.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes research-informed policy development and strategic foresight, aligning with 'Policy theme'. 'Innovation and technology policies' is included due to the mention of 'forecasts derived from research' and 'alternative future trends', which relate to technological and innovative foresight. Other categories like 'Curriculum' are excluded as the focus is on policy formulation, not curriculum content.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 25. Improving the use of resources. Governments and civil society are encouraged to find new and creative mechanisms to finance quality education for all young people (for example, by exploring the possibilities of converting debt repayments into investments in education). This should go hand in hand with an increased capacity for innovation and transformation.

low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph mentions 'innovative financing mechanisms' and 'conversion of debt into education investments', aligning with 'Teaching and learning materials' through resource optimization. 'ICT and digital infrastructure' is implied by 'innovation and transformation'. 'Other' covers non-specific resource use. 'School buildings' and 'WASH' are excluded as not mentioned. 'Transport' and 'laboratories' are not referenced.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph mentions 'converting debt repayments into investments in education' and 'new and creative mechanisms to finance quality education,' which aligns with PPPs involving both public and private sectors. 'Public' and 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the focus is on collaborative mechanisms rather than exclusive ownership. TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 26. Promote alliances and partnerships at all levels. Incentive measures and appropriate legislation could facilitate the effective functioning of more efficient partnerships. Governments are encouraged to continue engaging stakeholders such as students, teachers, parents, NGOs, the media, social partners including teachers' unions, and other decision-makers. A cross-sectoral approach is desirable. UNESCO's role as the lead agency in enhancing the quality of education for all young people is essential.

'Legal frameworks' is noted but not a sub-category here. Other options like 'inclusive education' or 'digital literacy' are not directly supported.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph mentions 'appropriate legislation' and 'incentive measures' which directly relate to education laws and regulations. It also emphasizes stakeholder engagement with specific groups like students, teachers, NGOs, and unions, aligning with rights and obligations of stakeholders. Other categories like international conventions or legal disputes are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: PRIORITES D'ACTION PROPOSITIONS DE PRIORITÉS POUR L'ACTION EN VUE D'AMÉLIORER LA QUALITÉ DE L'ÉDUCATION DE TOUS LES JEUNES [ADDED]
paragraph: 27. Educate for sustainable development, peace, and social justice. Significant efforts must be made to implement effective strategies, policies, and practices to ensure quality education for all young people. the United Nations Decade of Education for Sustainable Development and the United Nations Literacy Decade should provide unique opportunities to strengthen efforts in this area.

paragraph: 28. This document, resulting from the work of the 47th session of the International Conference on Education, will be taken into account to enrich reflection and strengthen, in the short, medium, and long term, the action programs of UNESCO, its International Bureau of Education, and its other specialized institutes, with a view to contributing to the improvement of the quality of education for all faiths.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: Gathered for the 48th session of the International Conference on Education (Geneva, 25-28 November 2008), we, Ministers of Education, heads of delegation, and delegates from 153 member States, alongside representatives of 20 intergovernmental organizations, 25 NGOs, foundations, and other civil society institutions, participated in constructive and stimulating discussions on the theme "Education for Inclusion: The Way of the Future."

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'Education for Inclusion' as the theme, directly aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity'. 'Other policy theme' is selected due to the debate's emphasis on categorization challenges and the theme's dual nature as both a policy and cross-cutting theme, which doesn't fit neatly into existing categories.

The summary highlights the dual nature of the theme as both policy and cross-cutting, justifying 'Other cross-cutting theme' to capture its interdisciplinary relevance. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy are not mentioned in the paragraph.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions Ministers of Education, heads of delegation, and member States (government/policy makers), NGOs, foundations, and civil society institutions (civil society/NGOs), and intergovernmental organizations (international organizations). These stakeholders are directly referenced, aligning with the 'Stakeholder focus' high-level label. Other categories like teachers, learners, or private sector are not mentioned, so they are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes 'Education for Inclusion' as a thematic focus, aligning with 'General (academic)' as it addresses broad educational principles. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is excluded due to lack of specific technical or vocational training references. 'Combined general and vocational' is not selected as the paragraph does not mention integration of vocational elements.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: At the conclusion of our work, the participants recalled Article 26 of the United Nations Universal Declaration of Human Rights, which states that everyone has the right to education.

'Legal frameworks' is the high-level label, so sub-labels must align with its scope. 'Cross-cutting themes' was excluded per the summary's guidance, and no policy implementation or stakeholder obligations were mentioned.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: We affirm that quality inclusive education for all is essential to ensure human, social, and economic development.

'Other cross-cutting theme' is included to capture the broader developmental emphasis not fully covered by specific categories. Categories like 'Human rights and equality' and 'Sustainable development and climate action' are excluded as the text does not explicitly link to these themes.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes 'quality inclusive education for all' which aligns with 'All learners'. 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' is included as 'quality education for all' implies addressing disparities, a common framing in UN resolutions. Other categories like 'Children' or 'Disabilities' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes 'quality inclusive education for all' as essential to development, which aligns with general academic education as a foundational component. Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as the focus is on broad education rather than specific skills. TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: We unanimously agree that governments, like all other social actors, have an important role to play in providing quality education for all, and in doing so, should recognize that it is essential to have an expanded concept of inclusive education that can address the diverse needs of all learners and is both relevant, equitable, and effective.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes an expanded concept of inclusive education addressing diverse learner needs, which aligns with general academic frameworks. Vocational/technical (TVET) is excluded as the focus is on inclusive education rather than specific skills training. It also highlights governments' roles in providing education, fitting 'Governance and planning'. 'Quality and learning outcomes' is partially relevant but not explicitly mentioned. 'Other policy theme' is avoided due to specific categories matching the text.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions governments as key stakeholders responsible for inclusive education, supporting 'Government and policy makers'. It also references 'diverse needs of all learners', directly linking to 'Learners and students'. TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: All forecasts indicate that the global financial crisis will have disproportionate effects on the poor, who are the least responsible for this situation.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: In this context, we reaffirm the importance of inclusive education to combat poverty, improve health, income, and livelihoods.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: Therefore, despite the current global financial crisis, we emphasize the need to give the highest priority to the financing of education and not to use this financial crisis as a justification for reducing resources allocated to education at both the national and international levels.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions prioritizing education financing during a financial crisis, directly aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'Education in emergencies and crises' is relevant as the crisis context is framed as a challenge requiring maintained educational investment. Other categories like 'Policy theme' are excluded as the focus is on specific financing actions rather than overarching policy frameworks.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: Drawing on the outcomes of the nine preparatory meetings and the four regional conferences on education for inclusion organized by the UNESCO International Bureau of Education, and based on the results of the plenary sessions and workshop deliberations of this Conference, we call upon Member States to adopt the approach of education for inclusion in the design, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation of educational policies, as a means to further accelerate the achievement of the Education for All (EFA) goals and thereby contribute to the construction of more inclusive societies.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes policy design, implementation, and monitoring (governance/planning) and explicitly mentions 'education for inclusion' (inclusion/diversity). Other categories like 'international cooperation' or 'lifelong learning' are not directly mentioned. 'Policy theme' is the high-level label, so sub-labels focus on specific policy aspects rather than broader cross-cutting themes.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'education for inclusion' and links it to policy design/implementation, aligning with 'Inclusive education and special needs'. The reference to 'more inclusive societies' and broader societal implications supports 'Other cross-cutting theme'. Categories like 'Human rights and equality' are indirectly related but not explicitly stated. 'Policy theme' is debated in the summary but not a permitted category.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes education for inclusion as a policy approach to achieve EFA goals, focusing on systemic design and societal impact. 'General (academic)' aligns with its broad policy and educational framework focus. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as there's no mention of vocational training. 'Vocational/technical (TVET)' is not supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'monitoring, and evaluation of educational policies' which directly aligns with the high-level label. Other categories like 'Education statistics and indicators' or 'EMIS and data systems' are not explicitly referenced. The focus on policy design and implementation ties more closely to monitoring and evaluation than to other sub-labels.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: To this end, the expanded concept of education for inclusion can be envisaged as an overarching guiding principle aimed at strengthening education for sustainable development and lifelong learning for all, as well as ensuring equal access for all segments of society to learning opportunities, in order to implement the principles of education for inclusion.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes education for sustainable development and lifelong learning, which aligns with general academic orientation. low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes inclusion and holistic development, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity'. 'Education orientation' and 'Cross-cutting themes' from the summary are not direct categories, so 'Inclusion and diversity' is prioritized over 'Other policy theme'.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 13. Strengthen the use of ICT in order to ensure broader access to learning opportunities, particularly in rural, remote, and disadvantaged areas.

low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph emphasizes using ICT to enhance access to learning opportunities, aligning with lifelong learning's focus on continuous access. While informal learning could be relevant, the emphasis on infrastructure and access points more toward lifelong learning. Formal and non-formal education are too narrow, and other categories are not supported.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph explicitly mentions strengthening ICT to ensure access to learning, directly aligning with 'ICT and digital infrastructure'. Other categories like school buildings or classrooms are not mentioned. The orchestrator summary highlights tensions between ICT access and pedagogy, reinforcing ICT's role under 'Infrastructure & resources'.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'rural, remote, and disadvantaged areas,' directly aligning with 'Rural and remote learners' and 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups.' Other categories like 'Children' or 'Youth' are not specified, and 'ICT' is discussed as a tool, not a learner group.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 14. Provide the opportunity to acquire high-quality non-formal education that leads to the formal recognition of skills acquired in non-formal settings.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes formal recognition of skills from non-formal settings, aligning with vocational/technical (TVET) focus. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the emphasis is on skill acquisition rather than general academic learning. 'Combined general and vocational' is not selected as the paragraph does not mention general education components.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes acquiring skills in non-formal settings leading to formal recognition, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' through skills acquisition. 'Inclusive education and special needs' is included as non-formal education often addresses marginalized groups. Other categories like digital literacy or human rights are not explicitly mentioned, and the focus on recognition over delivery methods excludes 'learning modality'.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 15. Increase efforts to reduce illiteracy as a mechanism of inclusion, taking into account the important role that educated parents can play in the education of their children.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes reducing illiteracy as a mechanism of inclusion, aligning with 'Inclusive education and special needs' through systemic inclusion. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is selected due to the focus on systemic change and parental role, which doesn't fit neatly into existing categories. Categories like 'Citizenship' or 'Human rights' are excluded as the text lacks explicit references to those themes.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions the role of educated parents in reducing illiteracy, directly linking parental education to inclusion. While 'communities' is broader, 'Parents and communities' is the most precise fit among allowed categories. Other categories like 'Government and policy makers' or 'Teachers and educators' are not directly referenced.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 16. Strengthen the role of teachers by striving to improve their status and working conditions, and develop mechanisms to recruit qualified candidates and retain competent teachers who are open to the diverse demands of learning.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph mentions mechanisms to recruit qualified candidates (Recruitment and hiring), improving working conditions (Working conditions and salaries), and developing mechanisms to retain competent teachers (Professional development). Categories like 'Qualification and certification' and 'Teacher supply and shortages' are not explicitly addressed, while 'Legal status and rights' is not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph directly addresses improving teachers' status and working conditions, aligning with 'Teachers and workforce policies'. The mention of 'diverse demands of learning' supports 'Inclusion and diversity'. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Financing' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Cross-cutting themes' and 'Stakeholder focus' from the summary are not direct matches to the paragraph's content.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes improving teacher adaptability to diverse learning demands, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' for adaptability skills. 'Inclusive education and special needs' is included due to the mention of diverse demands implying inclusivity. Other categories like 'Citizenship' or 'Human rights' are not explicitly addressed. 'Cross-cutting theme' was excluded as the focus is on teacher-specific skills rather than broader themes.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 17. Train teachers by providing them with the appropriate skills and materials to teach diverse groups of students and to meet the varied learning needs of learners through methods such as school-based professional development, initial training related to inclusion, and instruction attentive to the development and strengths of each learner.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'initial training related to inclusion' and 'school-based professional development', directly aligning with 'Initial education and training' and 'Professional development'. 'Legal status and rights' and 'Teacher supply and shortages' are excluded as the text focuses on training methods, not legal frameworks or workforce numbers.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes teaching diverse groups and meeting varied learning needs, aligning with 'Inclusive education and special needs'. Mention of methods attentive to learner development and strengths supports 'Life skills and 21st century competencies'. Other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Infrastructure' are excluded as the focus is on teaching methods and learner diversity, not content or resources.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes diverse learners, including those with disabilities (explicitly mentioned), Indigenous/ethnic minorities (implied by 'diverse groups'), and low-income/disadvantaged learners (implied by 'varied learning needs'). 'All learners' is included as a broad category encompassing these groups. 'Children (0-17)' and 'Youth and adolescents' are excluded as the age range isn't specified. 'Population in conflict' and 'Migrants' are not mentioned. 'Adults' are not explicitly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes methods like inclusion-focused training and learner-centered instruction, aligning with 'Teaching methods and approaches'. Mention of school-based professional development supports 'Professional learning communities'. 'Pedagogical foundations' is less direct, and other categories lack explicit evidence.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 18. Strengthen the strategic role of higher education in the initial and ongoing training of teachers regarding inclusive education practices, notably by providing sufficient resources.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph focuses on higher education's role in teacher training for inclusive practices, aligning with 'Higher education unspecified' as it encompasses all tertiary levels. Other categories like primary or secondary education are excluded since the text specifically references teacher training and resource allocation at the higher education level.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes strengthening higher education's role in initial teacher training and ongoing professional development for inclusive education. 'Initial education and training' matches the initial training context mentioned in the orchestrator summary. 'Professional development' aligns with 'ongoing training' and 'policy theme' discussions. Other categories like 'recruitment' or 'working conditions' are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes providing resources for teacher training in inclusive education, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' (resource allocation for training). 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' fits as it relates to developing skills (inclusive practices) through education. 'Design and development' is excluded as the focus is on implementation, not creation. 'Content and learning areas' is too narrow, and 'Evaluation and reform' is not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on teacher training (Teachers and workforce policies) and improving educational quality through inclusive practices (Quality and learning outcomes). 'Inclusion and diversity' is directly mentioned. 'Financing and resource mobilization' is excluded as the text emphasizes resource provision rather than funding mechanisms. 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' is not selected due to the debate context favoring 'Teachers' over 'Curriculum'.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 19. Encourage innovative research on teaching and learning methods related to inclusive education.

low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph mentions 'innovative research on teaching and learning methods' which directly relates to teaching methods and approaches. 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' is included as research on teaching methods inherently involves theoretical underpinnings. 'Other pedagogy related issue' is excluded as the focus is specifically on methods, not broader pedagogical issues. 'Assessment and feedback' is not mentioned, and the paragraph does not discuss professional learning communities or technology-supported pedagogy.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 20. Equip school administrators with the necessary skills to effectively respond to the diverse needs of all learners and to promote education for inclusion within their institutions.

low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes equipping administrators to address diverse learner needs and promote inclusion, aligning with 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits as it relates to developing necessary skills for effective administration. Other categories like 'Human rights and equality' were excluded as the focus is on institutional inclusion rather than rights framing.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on equipping school administrators with skills to address diverse learner needs and promote inclusion, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity'. 'Governance and planning' applies as it involves institutional leadership and policy implementation. Other categories like 'Teachers and workforce policies' are excluded as the focus is on administrators, not teaching staff. 'Education orientation' and 'Pedagogy & assessment' are not directly addressed.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 21. Take into account the protection of learners, teachers, and educational institutions during periods of conflict.

low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'learners, teachers, and educational institutions' as direct stakeholders needing protection during conflict. 'Teachers and educators' and 'Learners and students' are explicitly named. 'Educational institutions' is included as a third entity. Other categories like 'Government and policy makers' or 'International organizations' are not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph focuses on protecting learners, teachers, and institutions during conflict, which aligns with 'Other cross-cutting theme' as it addresses a specific, context-dependent risk rather than fitting neatly into predefined categories like human rights or civic education. Other categories like 'Global citizenship' or 'Sustainable development' are too broad or unrelated to direct conflict protection. The orchestrator summary highlights the need for a category that captures universal relevance across contexts, which 'Other cross-cutting theme' fulfills.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 22. Recognize UNESCO's leadership role in the field of education for inclusion through the following actions: • Encourage exchanges and dissemination of good practices; • Provide countries, upon request, with advice on how they can develop and implement policies for education for inclusion; • Promote South-South and North-South-South cooperation to advance education for inclusion; • Support efforts to increase resources allocated to education at both national and international levels; • Make special efforts to assist the Least Developed Countries and countries affected by conflicts in the implementation of these recommendations.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes education for inclusion, directly linking to 'Inclusion and diversity'. It mentions UNESCO's leadership and cooperation mechanisms like South-South partnerships, supporting 'International cooperation and partnerships'. Resource allocation and support for LDCs and conflict-affected countries align with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'Access and equity' is related but not explicitly mentioned. 'Education in emergencies and crises' is partially relevant but not directly stated.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes UNESCO's leadership (international organizations) and policy development (government/policymakers). It mentions supporting countries (governments) and special efforts for vulnerable groups (parents/communities). Civil society/NGOs and private sector are not explicitly referenced, while learners are not directly mentioned as stakeholders in the actions listed.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 23. Call upon other international organizations to also provide support to Member States, based on their needs, for the implementation of these recommendations.

low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph calls for international organizations to provide support to Member States, implying collaboration between international bodies (public) and states (public), but not explicitly private entities. 'Public-private partnership' captures the collaborative support mechanism. Other categories like 'Private non-profit' or 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the text does not mention private sector involvement. 'Community or faith-based' is irrelevant here.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly calls for international organizations to support Member States, aligning with 'International cooperation and partnerships'. The high-level label 'Policy theme' is directly applicable as the text outlines a policy-driven call to action. 'Other policy theme' was excluded due to the clear fit with existing categories.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'international organizations' being called upon to support Member States, directly aligning with 'International organizations and donors'. The high-level label 'Stakeholder focus' is reinforced by the emphasis on collaborative support mechanisms and stakeholder roles in implementation.

TITLE: RECOMMANDATIONS CONCERNANT L'EDUCATION POUR L'INCLUSION [ADDED]
paragraph: 24. Disseminate the Conclusions and recommendations unanimously adopted at the closing of the work of the forty-eighth session of the International Conference on Education by the actors and partners of the international educational community, in order to inspire, guide, support, and develop renewed and resolutely inclusive educational policies.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes inclusive educational policies, aligning with 'Inclusion and diversity'. 'International cooperation and partnerships' is supported by the mention of disseminating recommendations to the international educational community. 'Policy theme' is directly referenced in the orchestrator summary. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Financing and resource mobilization' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph mentions disseminating recommendations to 'actors and partners of the international educational community,' aligning with 'International organizations and donors.' 'Government and policy makers' are directly targeted as the recipients of policy guidance. Other categories like 'Civil society and NGOs' or 'Private sector and industry' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' is not directly referenced.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes 'renewed and resolutely inclusive educational policies' directly aligning with 'Inclusive education and special needs'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is selected due to the broad policy dissemination and international collaboration mentioned, which don't fit other specific categories.
